programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Pearfield B&B in Petersfield, Hampshire, Cathy Robinson and her son Alex hope to impress their guests with a home-from-home experience. But are Cathy's rivals out for revenge.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid and who's won.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In expensive Surrey, Kirstie meets Lauren, who's moving from London out to Guildford, while Phil helps Eleanor, Laurence and their twin baby boys find the good life in Surbiton.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil Spencer revisits Sarah and Ian, whose Northamptonshire semi was bursting at the seams. Did Phil's wow-factor homes tempt them to list? And what did they do next.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Does good architecture have the power to heal? Kevin revisits Elinor and Born, who in 2017 built a clean-aired, hypoallergenic house, motivated by their two sons' extreme allergies.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Robert, who's 37, is taken to St George's after a motorbike collision. And 54-year-old Mary collapses in the street on her way to work.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Charulata, who's 59, is rushed to A&E after collapsing at home with signs of a stroke. Luke, who's 28, has cut his finger and is concerned that it could trigger a seizure.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance teams use battlefield dressings to try to save the life of a man who's bleeding to death. And nine-year-old Ar-Rayyan has been hit by a car.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Robert, who's 37, is taken to St George's after a motorbike collision. And 54-year-old Mary collapses in the street on her way to work.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Charulata, who's 59, is rushed to A&E after collapsing at home with signs of a stroke. Luke, who's 28, has cut his finger and is concerned that it could trigger a seizure.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped's South Korean Adventure
programme.desc: From Seoul to Gwangju, and from kimchi to Korean Fried Chicken and spam, Kate Quilton and John Whaite unearth the origins of the Korean foods that are exploding in popularity.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien unearth a mystery item that looks like a fish tank but turns out to have a different use entirely.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, Henry Cole uncovers an anvil and Simon O'Brien finds an old dresser, which he thinks could become a wildlife sanctuary for birds and insects.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jennifer McGirr hosts at Tower View Hotel in Blackpool, where there are unwanted guests in a bathroom and trouble at breakfast time when some of Jennifer's guests try to order off-menu.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Daniel welcomes his rivals to Church Spa in East Markham, Nottinghamshire. Some of his guests are impressed by the spa facilities, while others are less smitten with the price tag.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Lisa host at the King's Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex. Their guests get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone has a solid night's sleep.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former GMTV presenter Matt Arnold hosts at the Bridge Hotel in Brandon, Suffolk, where he hopes his 'blissful beds, beautiful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts' will impress his guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers meet for one last time, to confront their rivals and discover how much they've been paid. Things get heated when it's time to count up the cash.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host, alarm company owner Bill, plans a belt-busting menu for his guests, including the alarmingly named 'death by peanut butter' dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Architectural technician Alex wants to give his guests an education in fine dining, with a complex menu and fine wines, and introduces them to his 'fur babies'.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cat-loving vegetarian Hannah serves up an Italian menu and has a cunning plan to win over her meat-eating guests.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-styled food critic Phil has been less than impressed with the standard of food so far and has high hopes for his night. But he makes a schoolboy error.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kim plans a 'Yorkshire-themed' menu - featuring dishes from Spain and Thailand - accompanied by a Scottish choir singing American songs.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Marianne and her housekeeper Fran welcome their rivals to Langsmeade House in Oxfordshire, where their quirky hosting and 1920s knick-knacks are a hit, but there's a surprise at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Natasha and Dai host at the Stratford House guest house in Stratford-upon-Avon, but the family-friendly vibe doesn't gel with all their guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Michael and his mum Lorna host at Edmund House in Deal, Kent. One guest is less than thrilled by the 'no-frills' experience, and others don't fancy the activity: competitive dog grooming.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark hosts at the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire. But his guests don't all share his passion for beer - and is he too honest for his own good.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers meet for one last time to discover how much they've been paid, settle some scores and find out who's won.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Southampton, Kirstie helps Antoinette and Matt revive their attempts to get on the property ladder. And Phil's with Catherine who wants a fresh start in charming Chichester.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: The Rowland family's pokey three-bed in Stoke lacks kerb appeal but Allen loves the area. Can Kirstie and Phil end the family's decade-long debate: spruce up or sell.

programme.title: The Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: It's time for sweet treats and psychoanalysis as the Express pulls into Vienna. In Budapest, City of Spas, there's a high-speed rail line run entirely by kids.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tom, who's 13, is rushed to St George's after crashing his bike headfirst into a parked car while racing a friend. Andy, who's 26, has injured his knee while playing Frisbee.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gerry, who's 75, is rushed to A&E with a dangerously high heart rate of over 250 BPM. Six-year-old Daniel has an infected blister on his foot.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance teams race to help farmer Dennis, who's been pierced in the chest by a large metal spike, and motorcyclist Ian, who's crashed in the Lake District.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tom, who's 13, is rushed to St George's after crashing his bike headfirst into a parked car while racing a friend. Andy, who's 26, has injured his knee while playing Frisbee.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gerry, who's 75, is rushed to A&E with a dangerously high heart rate of over 250 BPM. Six-year-old Daniel has an infected blister on his foot.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits McVitie's to ask why biscuits have little holes all over them. Jimmy Doherty finds out how wine bottle corks are made.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Discover Must Have Ideas: Shopping
programme.desc: Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some Gothic church windows.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Wales, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jane and Amy host the first visit at Wisteria House in Hampshire. One guest gets picky at breakfast and 25-year-old Zak surprises his rivals when he reveals his hospitality experience.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Zak hopes to make his guests feel at home at The Living Room B&B in Bournemouth. But Jane and Amy encounter an eight-legged intruder, and David doesn't hold back at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Lewis welcomes his rivals to The Valley Hotel on Anglesey, where there's an unwanted visitor in one of the bedrooms, and criticism leaves Mike looking for answers.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira host at Barratt's at Ty'n Rhyl in North Wales, where there are hairy encounters for each of their guests. And the feedback leaves the hosts feeling distraught.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: With the visits over, the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their rivals, settle some cleanliness concerns and discover how much they've been paid, before the winner is announced.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time rapper Warren waxes lyrical while he cooks his Spanish cuisine, and treats his guests to a musical interlude.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Extremely competitive entrepreneur Donna plans a colourful evening of delicious food and relaxed fun.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamour model and columnist Susie is out to prove that she's not the 'dizzy blonde' her guests think, and they discover there's more to Susie than meets the eye..

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Straight-talking businesswoman Vicki, who's been very direct with her rival diners, surprises her guests by reading out a heartfelt poem she's written about them.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Veterinary nurse Robin hosts the final night with an animal theme and well-presented cuisine. Is he the dark horse of the competition.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan host at Trecarne House in Liskeard, Cornwall. Guest Dominic is impressed by his room with a view, but Stephen and Joanna make dusty discoveries.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Captain Dominic Read hosts at the Magna Carta, a five-star floating B&B in Windsor, where some guests are bowled over, but others take the wind out of his sails with their criticism.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Kent, Rayne and Chris hope that Ash Cottage's unique decor will impress their guests. But will they tie themselves in knots with their rope-making activity.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stephen Bull and his housekeeper Joanna Harris hope to wow their guests with a top-notch experience at their Brighton hotel, A Room with a View. But the wheels come off at breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their critics and find out how much they've been paid and who's won.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: We catch up with Steph and Mark, who have turned their Nottingham nest into every inch the family home. And did stylish pair Elliot and James achieve their Windsor wonderland.
